message:
You know that moment when the weather forecast is calling for a big storm, and you're trying to keep the kids calm while secretly feeling a bit frazzled yourself? Last winter, as snow piled up outside, my kids were buzzing with excitement, convinced they were going to be snowed in for days. Meanwhile, I was mentally calculating how many snacks we had left and if we could make it through without a trip to the store!

So, I turned it into a family adventure. We made a game out of prepping — creating our own ‘storm kit’ together. The kids helped gather flashlights, snacks, and their favorite board games. It turned into a fun bonding time and eased a lot of my tension. Plus, they learned a little about being ready for whatever weather comes our way. 

It’s funny how a little preparation can turn anxiety into excitement. Have you had a similar experience with your kids? What fun ways have you found to prep together?
